title: Light distribution structure and element, related method and uses
abstract: A light distribution structure 10 and a related element 100, such as a light guide, are provided. The structure 10 is preferably an optically functional layer comprising an at least one feature pattern 11, 11A established in a light-transmitting carrier by a plurality of three-dimensional optical features variable in terms of at least one of the cross-sectional profile, dimensions, periodicity, orientation and disposition thereof within the feature pattern. In some instances, the optical features are embodied as internal optical cavities 12 capable to establish the total internal reflection (TIR) function at a horizontal surface and at an essentially vertical surface thereof. A method for manufacturing the light distribution structure is further provided.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A light distribution structure (10) in the form of an optically functional layer comprising an at least one three-dimensional feature pattern (11, 11 A) established in a light- transmitting carrier medium by a plurality of internal optical cavities (12), wherein each said optical cavity (12) is configured to establish an at least one optical function at its' horizontal surface (121) and at its' essentially vertical surface (122), the latter being arranged in a direction of light propagation in the light-transmitting carrier medium, wherein - by said horizontal surface (121) the optical cavity (12) is configured to mediate light propagation in the light-transmitting carrier medium along an essentially longitudinal light propagation path, and to further distribute light rays (51) reflected from said horizontal surface, in the light-transmitting carrier medium, towards the essen- tially vertical surface of the subsequent optical cavity of cavities (12) via a plurality of light passages (13), and - by said essentially vertical surface (122) the optical cavity (12) is configured to extract light (52) out of the structure (10) in a predetermined direction essentially transverse to the longitudinal light propagation path, and wherein said at least one optical function is established by a configuration of the light passage area (13) combined with an at least one of the dimensions, periodicity, orientation and disposition of the optical cavities (12) within the feature pattern (11, 11 A). 2 The light distribution structure (10) of claim 1, wherein said at least one optical function is the total internal reflection (TIR) function. 3. The light distribution structure (10) of any one of claims 1 or 2, wherein each optical cavity (12) is configured to receive and to further distribute light arriving thereto at an angle of incidence equal to or greater than the critical angle relative to the surface normal. 4. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, wherein the established optical cavities (12) are filled with a gaseous material, such as air. 5. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, wherein each individual optical cavity (12) is configured variable in terms of at least one of the cross-sectional profile, dimensions, periodicity, orientation and disposition thereof within the feature pattern. 6. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, in which the optical cavities (12) are established with the three-dimensional profiles selected from one of the essentially blazed, curved or wave-shaped profiles. 7. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, in which in which the op- tical cavities (12) are established with the three-dimensional profiles provided as symmetrical sinusoidal waveforms or asymmetrical sinusoidal waveforms. 8. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, wherein cross-sectional profile variability, curvature angle variability and /or curvature radius variability is established, for each individual optical cavity (12), with a predetermined periodicity. 9. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, wherein the at least one feature pattern (11, 11 A) comprises a plurality of optical cavities (12) with discrete profiles or at least partly continuous profiles. 10. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, wherein cross-sectional variability is established, for each individual optical cavity (12), in three dimensions by the at least one of the: curvature angle, curvature radius, length of a pitch, width, height, period, phase, configuration of a light passage area (13), and the like. 11. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, wherein the feature pattern is configured to extend over the entire optically functional layer. 12. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim 1-10, comprising a num- ber of feature patterns arranged on an at least one optically functional layer according to a predetermined order. 13. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, wherein, within the at least one feature pattern, the plurality of optical cavities (12) is arranged into an array or arrays extending along and/or across an entire area occupied by said feature pattern. 14. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, wherein the optically functional layer has a fill factor for the feature pattern equal to 100% or less than 100%. 15. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, comprising at least two optically functional layers with the at least one feature pattern established on each said layer. 16. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, provided in the form of a film, a sheet or a coating. 17. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, wherein the optically functional layer is established in an optical polymer or glass. 18. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim, wherein the optical cavities (12) are formed at an interface with an additional flat, planar carrier layer, selected from a transparent layer, a reflector layer, and/or a coloured layer. 19. The light distribution structure (10) of any preceding claim configured to receive light from a plurality of point light sources. 20. A method for manufacturing a light distribution structure (10) in the form of an optically functional layer comprising an at least one feature pattern (11, 11 A) established in a light-transmitting carrier by a plurality of three-dimensional optical features variable in terms of at least one of the cross-sectional profile, dimensions, periodicity, orientation and disposition thereof within the feature pattern, said method comprises: - manufacturing a patterned master tool for said three-dimensional feature pattern (11, 11 A) by a piezoelectric cutting method selected from a fast tool servo (FTS) method and a stylus engraving method, or by a laser engraving method, and - transferring the three-dimensional feature pattern (11, 11 A) onto the light-transmitting carrier. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the step of manufacturing the patterned master tool comprises provision of the three-dimensional feature pattern (11, 11 A) configured as symmetrical or asymmetrical sinusoidal waveforms. 22. The method of any one of claims 20 or 21, wherein the master tool is fabricated in a planar or a cylindrical format. 23. The method of any preceding claim 20-22, wherein the step of transferring the three-dimensional feature pattern onto the light-transmitting carrier is implemented by a roll-to-roll method, a roll-to-sheet method or a sheet-to- sheet method. 24. The method of any preceding claims 20-23, further comprising a surface polishing treatment. 25. A light distribution element (100), comprising an optically transparent substrate configured to establish a path for light propagation therealong, and an at least one light distribution structure (10), as defined in any one of claims 1-19. 26. The light distribution element (100) of claim 25, comprising the light distribution structure (10) in the form of an additional layer, such as a film, a sheet or a coating, disposed on an at least one surface of the optically transparent substrate. 27. The light distribution element (100) of claim 25, comprising the light distribution structure (10) fully integrated and/or embedded into the optically transparent substrate. 28. The light distribution element (100) of any preceding claims 25-27, configured as a light guide, a light pipe, a light-guide film or a light-guide plate. 29. The light distribution element (100) of any preceding claims 25-28, further comprising an at least one light source, selected from: a Light Emitting Diode (LED), an Organic Light Emitting Diode (OLED), a laser diode, a LED bar, an OLED strip, a microchip LED strip, and a cold cathode tube. 30. Use of the light distribution element (100, 100A) as defined in any one of claim 25- 29 in illumination and indication. 31. Use according to claim 30, in illumination of wall- and roof panels, in window and facade illumination, in signage illumination, in greenhouse illumination, in display illumination, in passive matrix illumination, in signal illumination, in touch signal solutions, in security systems, in producing light-directing films, in creating light masks for a non-transparent mode, in security systems, in indicator devices, in reflectors, and/or in light collector solutions. 32. A roll of a light distribution element comprising: an optically functional layer comprising an at least one feature pattern (11, 11 A) established in a light-transmitting carrier by a plurality of three-dimensional optical features variable in terms of at least one of the cross-sectional profile, dimensions, periodicity, orientation and disposition thereof within the feature pattern, and a light filter layer (141) formed of a substrate material having a refractive index than the refractive index lower that the refractive index of the carrier medium constituting the optically functional layer, and comprising a plurality of apertures. 33. The roll of a light distribution element, according to claim 32, in which the optically functional layer is established by the structure, as defined in any one of claims 1-19.
